data.title: Gabriel De Prado named President, U.S. Market of Haypp Group, effective January 9, 2026. Former CCO will lead operations.
data.description: The parent company of Nicokick.com and Northerner.com, Haypp Group, has appointed Gabriel De Prado as the President of the U.S. market starting January 9, 2026. De Prado, former Chief Commercial Officer, will be responsible for overseeing the company's business operations in the U.S. His key focus areas will involve enhancing commercial strategies, increasing regulatory involvement, and fortifying the company's position in the market.

data.title: Growing Connected Gym Equipment Market fueled by digital fitness adoption, smart training tech, and evolving wellness trends.
data.description: The market for Connected Gym Equipment is experiencing significant growth, driven by the increasing adoption of smart fitness solutions that incorporate hardware, software, and data-driven insights. This trend is fueled by a growing health consciousness among consumers, the expansion of home fitness ecosystems, and the integration of emerging technologies such as AI, IoT, and cloud-based solutions. The industry is projected to reach a market value of approximately [insert specific value] by [insert specific year], reflecting the strong momentum in this sector.

data.title: U.S. enterprises are advancing analytics and AI, transitioning to integrated programs from isolated initiatives: ISG.
data.description: In Stamford, Connecticut, large U.S. enterprises are progressing in their advanced analytics and artificial intelligence (AI) efforts, moving from standalone projects to comprehensive, integrated programs, according to ISG. This shift signifies a maturation in their strategies, highlighting a move towards a more holistic approach to analytics and AI. The transition reflects a growing trend among businesses to leverage these technologies more strategically and at a larger scale.

data.title: DOGE loses $39.29M as it collapses by 49% from peak, lacks buying interest; big holders actively selling, red flag.
data.description: Dogecoin experienced its largest single-day outflow in months, with $39.29 million exiting the market on Friday. This significant exit coincided with both Dogecoin and Shiba Inu breaking key support levels, leading to zero buying interest. Currently, Dogecoin is down 2% and has plummeted by 49% from its peak of $0.27 in September. The $39.29 million outflow is seen as a concerning indicator, suggesting that major holders are actively selling their positions. The chart displays a concerning trend of aggressive exits, with the spike in outflows on Friday being particularly worrisome. Additionally, Dogecoin spot ETFs have also been impacted by these developments.

data.title: Alaska Communications appoints Jesse Kleinbach as VP of carrier & wholesale services, focusing on value for partners.
data.description: Alaska Communications in Anchorage has ramped up its carrier and wholesale services division for increased partner and customer benefits by naming Jesse Kleinbach as the new vice president. With over 25 years of experience in the telecom industry, Kleinbach excels in developing partner-focused solutions. His track record includes spearheading projects that streamline connectivity, broaden market potential, and guarantee clients access to dependable, cutting-edge services.

data.title: Evernote releases v11 with significant AI improvements after 5 years, including AI Assistant, Semantic Search, and AI Meeting Notes, available to all users.
data.description: In Milan, Evernote unveiled v11, marking its first significant product update in five years. This release introduces three advanced AI functionalities: AI Assistant, Semantic Search, and AI Meeting Notes. These features aim to enhance the efficiency of information capture, retrieval, and management within Evernote. Following two years of development, v11 is now accessible to all Evernote users, showcasing the company's evolution under Bending Spoons.

China continues to dump US Treasuries China continues to reduce its US Treasury holdings. In November, its holdings fell by $6.1 billion to $682.6 billion, the lowest level since September 2008 during the global financial crisis.

data.title: JAGGAER India earns 2025 Great Place To Work Certification with 97% employee satisfaction.
data.description: Located in Hyderabad, India, JAGGAER India has achieved certification as a Great Place To Work for 2025. The certification, granted by Great Place To Work®, is a significant accomplishment based on feedback from current employees at JAGGAER. Impressively, 97% of employees deemed it a great workplace. Great Place To Work® is renowned globally for its expertise in workplace culture, employee experience, and effective leadership strategies that drive superior revenue, employee retention rates, and overall growth.

data.title: Fly Alliance Maintenance expands with DGCA approval in India to work on Indian-registered aircraft, boosting global capabilities.
data.description: Fly Alliance Maintenance, the maintenance branch of Fly Alliance, has expanded its global maintenance capabilities by obtaining approval as a Foreign Approved Maintenance Organization (FAMO) from the Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA) of India. This new official recognition allows Fly Alliance Maintenance to conduct maintenance services on Indian-registered aircraft, specifically those with VT tail numbers. This approval marks a significant milestone for the company, enhancing its international presence and reinforcing its commitment to providing quality maintenance services worldwide.
